message:
fix(messaging): correct json_escape to use Unicode code points, not UTF-8 bytes

The json_escape() function in jetstream.rs was encoding control characters
by iterating over their UTF-8 byte representation and emitting per-byte
\u00XX escapes. For multi-byte control chars (e.g. U+0080), this produced
invalid JSON like \u00c2\u0080 instead of the correct \u0080.

Fixed to use `c as u32` for the Unicode code point directly, matching the
correct pattern already used in nats.rs::nats_json_escape().

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
